summary: Exceptional ultramatrix remaster captures a stellar second set – China Cat → Crazy Fingers → I Know You Rider, The Wheel → Gimme Some Lovin', and a soaring Black Muddy River encore.
review: The reviewer lauds the second set as outstanding, highlighting the seamless China Cat‑Crazy Fingers‑IKYR transition, the high‑energy Wheel‑Gimme Some Lovin' pairing and Jerry’s emotive Black Muddy River. The ultramatrix source is described as “glorious” with a full sonic display, though early‑set mix issues (undermixed Jerry and occasional Brent keyboard tone quirks) are noted.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review overwhelmingly praises the musical performance, especially the second set, while noting only minor recording mix issues; this yields a high overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Ultramatrix remaster praised as full‑sonic and exceptional; occasional early‑set mixing problems (Jerry’s guitar/vocals under‑mixed) and Brent’s organ/keyboard tone inconsistencies.

standout_songs: China Cat > Crazy Fingers > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts strong but the opening songs feel under‑whelming; second set is a high‑water mark with superb transitions, tight band interplay and an inspired encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Early vocal miscues and being undermixed in the first set; later loud enough and delivers an emotional Black Muddy River with his voice in top form.
band_member_comments.Phil: Steps up on Blow Away, drives the band on INYR, and provides massive, mile‑wide bass presence during The Wheel > Gimme Some Lovin'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ tone switch is shocking; at times his piano/organ sound misses the mark and sounds coarse/out of tune on Sugar Magnolia.
